scope.summary: Implement named timeline markers as a Vec<TimelineMarker> on the Timeline type, exposed in the animation authoring API and stored in tailor_animations, enabling section labeling, named-range export anchors, and timeline navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline type gains a markers: Vec<TimelineMarker> field where TimelineMarker carries { name: String, frame: u32 }.
Markers round-trip through tailor_animations animation_json JSONB without loss.
The animation authoring API (MT-294) exposes add_marker(name, frame) and remove_marker(name) calls.
A model can query markers by name and receive the frame index, enabling named-range export without hardcoded frame numbers.
The viewport timeline bar (MT-295) renders marker labels at their frame positions.
scope.risk_if_missed: Animation Range Export (the proposed MT below) cannot use named anchors, forcing callers to specify raw frame numbers. Editorial organization of multi-section animations is absent. MD 2026.0 timeline markers are a navigation-critical feature for any animation longer than a single gesture.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
